data: Welcome to the Aether Identity documentation. Aether Identity is an enterprise-grade OAuth2/OIDC authentication and secure account management solution.
data.getting-started#Getting Started: Learn how to integrate Aether Identity into your applications with our comprehensive guides and API references.
data.features#Features:
Enterprise OAuth2/OIDC - Secure authentication with industry standards
Multi-Factor Authentication - Enhanced security with MFA support
Identity Management - Complete user lifecycle management
Account Security - Advanced security features and monitoring
Cross-Platform SDKs - Support for Node.js, PHP, Go, and more
data.quick-links#Quick Links:
Deployment Guide
API Reference
SDK Documentation
data.support#Support: For support, please visit our GitHub Issues or Discussions.

data: This guide covers the deployment options for Aether Identity.
data.table-of-contents#Table of Contents:
Quick Start
Docker Deployment
Kubernetes Deployment
Environment Variables
data.quick-start#Quick Start:
The quickest way to get started with Aether Identity is using Docker Compose:
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/skygenesisenterprise/aether-identity.git
cd aether-identity
# Start with Docker Compose
docker-compose up -d
data.using-docker-compose#Using Docker Compos:
version: "3.8"
services:
  aether-identity:
    image: ghcr.io/skygenesisenterprise/aether-identity:latest
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    environment:
      - NODE_ENV=production
      - DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@db:5432/aether
    depends_on:
      - db
  db:
    image: postgres:15-alpine
    environment:
      - POSTGRES_USER=user
      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=pass
      - POSTGRES_DB=aether
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
volumes:
  postgres_data:
data.using-helm-recommended#Using Helm (Recom:
# Add the Helm repository
helm repo add aether https://charts.skygenesisenterprise.io
helm repo update
# Install Aether Identity
helm install aether-identity aether/aether-identity \
  --set database.host=postgresql \
  --set database.password=secretpassword
data.using-kubectl#Using kubectl:
# Apply the Kubernetes manifests
kubectl apply -f k8s/
Sample deployment manifest:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: aether-identity
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: aether-identity
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: aether-identity
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: aether-identity
          image: ghcr.io/skygenesisenterprise/aether-identity:latest
          ports:
            - containerPort: 3000
          env:
            - name: NODE_ENV
              value: "production"
            - name: DATABASE_URL
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: aether-secrets
                  key: database-url
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: aether-identity
spec:
  selector:
    app: aether-identity
  ports:
    - port: 80
      targetPort: 3000
data.required-variables#Required Variables: Variable	Description	Default	NODE_ENV	Environment mode	development	DATABASE_URL	PostgreSQL connection string	-	JWT_SECRET	Secret key for JWT signing	-	ENCRYPTION_KEY	Key for data encryption	-
data.optional-variables#Optional Variables: Variable	Description	Default	PORT	HTTP server port	3000	LOG_LEVEL	Logging level	info	REDIS_URL	Redis connection (for caching)	-	SMTP_HOST	SMTP server for emails	-	SMTP_PORT	SMTP port	587
data.oauth-configuration#OAuth Configuration: Variable	Description	OAUTH_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID	Google OAuth client ID	OAUTH_GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET	Google OAuth client secret	OAUTH_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID	GitHub OAuth client ID	OAUTH_GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET	GitHub OAuth client secret
data.production-checklist#Production Checklis:
Before deploying to production, ensure:
All secrets are properly configured
SSL/TLS certificates are installed
Database migrations are run
Backup strategy is in place
Monitoring and logging are configured
Rate limiting is enabled
Security headers are configured
data.common-issues#Common Issues:
Database Connection Failed
Error: connect ECONNREFUSED
Verify DATABASE_URL is correct
Ensure database is running and accessible
Check firewall rules
Port Already in Use
Error: listen EADDRINUSE: address already in use :::3000
Change PORT environment variable
Stop other services using port 3000
data.next-steps#Next Steps:
